nodes.label: ItemStock
nodes.description: This aspect represents the latest quantities of a partner's items that are on stock. The stock represent the build-to-order (BTO) stocks already available.

nodes.label: positions
nodes.description:
In case of a supplier's stocks for a customer, these stocks may reference the order position for that they have been produced. 
In case of a customer's stock delivered by a supplier, there is exactly one position.

In case there is no item on stock, positions MUST be empty.

nodes.label: materialNumberCustomer
nodes.description: Material identifier as assigned by customer. This material number identifies the material (as planned) in customer's database. For standard parts (e.g. screws) for which a customer does not have an own number, the materialNumberSupplier should be set in this field, too.

nodes.label: materialGlobalAssetId
nodes.preferredName: Material Number UUID of the Customer's Twin
nodes.description: Material identifier used uniquely to identify the Material (e.g. PartAsPlanned).

nodes.label: materialNumberSupplier
nodes.description: Material identifier as assigned by supplier. This material number identifies the material (as planned) in supplier's database.

nodes.label: direction
nodes.description: Direction of the stock from data provider perspective.

nodes.label: orderPositionReference
nodes.description: The order position reference contains information to identify a position within an order.

nodes.label: lastUpdatedOnDateTime
nodes.description: Date and time at which the position has been updated by the supplier's systems. Date and time are set according to a timezone.

nodes.label: allocatedStocks
nodes.description: Stocks that have been already allocated to the customer or delivered by a specific supplier. An allocated stock always refers to a stock location.

nodes.label: supplierOrderId
nodes.description: This is the order number on the supplier side that is given by e.g., the supplier's enterprise resource planning system.

nodes.label: customerOrderId
nodes.description: This is the order number on the customer side that can be given by e.g., the customer's enterprise resource planning system.

nodes.label: customerOrderPositionId
nodes.description: This is an identifier for the position of an order defined by the customer. It's not a technical identifier, such as an UUID. This identifier is used within the business context when talking about the position.

nodes.label: quantityOnAllocatedStock
nodes.description: This is the quantity of the material on stock. In case of stocks "from a supplier ready to be shipped to a certain customer", the stock may refer to an order position of a customer.

nodes.label: stockLocationBPNS
nodes.description: BPNS identifying the site the stock is located at. One stock has always one location.

nodes.label: isBlocked
nodes.description:
A set of finished items that are located on the blocked stock area, quarantine area, etc. waiting to be released.

Example: Stock is in review and not released due to customer complaint regarding a recent batch.

nodes.label: stockLocationBPNA
nodes.description: BPNA identifying the address the stock is located at. One stock has always one location.

nodes.label: PositionsCollection
nodes.description:
If any stock is available, then there is at least one position. 

In case of a supplier's stock for a customer, the supplier can  differentiate the stock according to the order positions of his customer. In this case multiple positions can be given. There could also be only one position without an OrderPositionReference indicating an anonymous stock which is allocated to the customer.

In case of a customer's stock delivered by a supplier, there is exactly one position without order position references.

nodes.label: AllocatedStockCollection
nodes.description: Collection of allocated stocks that may relate to different stock locations. Per location there could be at maximum one blocked and one non blocked stock at the same time.

nodes.label: MaterialNumberCharacteristic
nodes.description: The material number is a multi-character string, usually assigned by an ERP system.

nodes.label: OrderPositionReferenceCharacteristic
nodes.description: The order position reference includes reference numbers for the order and the ID. As these information are meant for the customer, the customer reference numbers are mandatory.

nodes.label: DateTimeStamp
nodes.description: The Date with the the timezone of the involved site.

nodes.label: OrderIdCharacteristic
nodes.description: This is a proprietary order ID, generated by either the supplier side or customer side.

nodes.label: OrderPositionIdCharacteristic
nodes.description: The position ID is unique for an order and used within business context to refer to a position.

nodes.label: IsBlockedCharacteristic
nodes.description: Boolean characteristic indicating whether a stock is blocked (true) or not (false).

nodes.label: DirectionCharacteristic
nodes.description:
If a supplier provides stock information to a customer, use "OUTBOUND".
If a customer provides stock information to a supplier, use "INBOUND".

nodes.label: Position
nodes.description:
The Position can be located at several stocks. 
In case of a supplier's stock for a customer, a position may be either anonymous or reference a position within a customer order. 
In case of a customer's stock for a supplier, the order position reference MUST NOT be set.

nodes.label: OrderPositionReference
nodes.description: Encapsulates the references to identify a position within an order.

nodes.label: AllocatedStock
nodes.description:
This is the quantity of items on stock at a location. A stock can either be
- from a certain supplier and ready to be consumed by a customer or
- from a supplier and ready to be shipped to a certain customer. 

In case of stocks "from a supplier ready to be shipped to a certain customer", the stock may refer to an order position of a customer. This stock consists only of the good-finished items.

nodes.label: QuantityCharacteristic
nodes.description: Describes the quantity on stock of the item defined by a quantity value and the measurement unit in which the quantity is measured.
